text:
Once upon a time in the quaint town of Maplewood, there lived an unusually shy squirrel named Oliver. He didn't speak very often and especially avoided loud birds. One unusually quiet autumn morning, Oliver heard sweet, melodic music weaving through the trees. His tiny nose twitched. It wasn't any bird he knew. It was a flute.

Curiosity got the better of him. He worked his small jaws together slowly. Gradually, his voice broke through the melody.

"D-o-o-lv-e? That's me!"

The flute played again. Oliver fluttered his big yellow eyes toward the tall oak near the stream. There sat a young girl music pupil named Lily. She didn't notice Oliver.

In moments like these, something inside him shimmered. He didn't hop closer. He just sat there, listening. Lily finished a piece. Realizing just owl she was, she gave a small happy smile.

"Just a little wandering creature," Oliver said softly.

"Nifty colors.
